issues.blurb: Daily commuting miles and weather wear the chain and cassette, and a neglected chain leads to skipping and faster drivetrain replacement.
Rim or disc brakes need regular pad and adjustment attention, since stop-and-go commuting eats through pads and detunes the setup.
Commuter tires pick up glass and debris on city streets, making flats one of the most frequent interruptions for riders.
Racks, fenders, and bolted-on accessories vibrate loose over time, causing rattles and the risk of parts shifting or dropping.
Hub, headset, and bottom bracket bearings wear and develop play from all-weather commuting and infrequent service.

narrative.headline: What hybrid / commuter bike owners report most
narrative.summary: These are issues commonly reported by hybrid and commuter riders as a general reference, not expert-verified data and possibly outdated. Drivetrain, brakes, and flats tend to dominate for bikes ridden in all weather.

issues.title: Drivetrain & Chain
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~26%) — commuting miles and weather wear the chain and cassette; neglect causes skipping.

issues.title: Brakes
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~22%) — stop-and-go commuting eats pads and detunes the brakes.

issues.title: Flats & Tires
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~22%) — city debris causes frequent flats and tire wear.

issues.title: Rack, Fender & Accessory Bolts Loosening
issues.blurb: Often reported (~18%) — racks, fenders, and accessories vibrate loose and rattle.

issues.title: Bearings
issues.blurb: Reported (~12%) — hub, headset, and bottom bracket bearings develop play from all-weather use.
